description:
You are a performance-engineer who has just received three raw profiling log files under
  /home/user/perf/raw/:

    • /home/user/perf/raw/app1.log  
    • /home/user/perf/raw/app2.log  
    • /home/user/perf/raw/app3.log  

  Each line inside those files already follows this exact pattern  
     [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] FUNC=<function-name> CPU_MS=<integer> MEM_MB=<integer>  

  Your job is to transform the raw data and build four new artefacts inside two *new*
  directories that you must create first:

    1.  /home/user/perf/processed/        (for all machine-readable files)  
    2.  /home/user/perf/reports/          (for the final human report)  

  Produce all four files **exactly** as described below – the automated grader will
  byte-compare them:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1.  /home/user/perf/processed/master.log  
      • Merge the contents of the three original *.log* files.  
      • Sort the lines strictly in ascending chronological order, based on the
        timestamp at the beginning of each line.  
      • Do **not** add or remove anything else: no header, no blank lines.  
      • The resulting file must therefore contain exactly 11 lines.

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  2.  /home/user/perf/processed/top_cpu.txt  
      • From the merged result, compute the cumulative CPU_MS per distinct FUNC.  
      • Take the five functions with the highest cumulative CPU_MS values.  
      • If two functions tie, the one whose name comes first in normal
        ASCIIbetical order wins the higher rank.  
      • Output format – exactly five lines, each in this comma-separated form  
            RANK,FUNCTION,CUMULATIVE_MS  
        where RANK is 1–5. No spaces anywhere in the line.

        Example of correct formatting (values will be different in the real run):  
            1,foo,123  
            2,bar,77

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  3.  /home/user/perf/processed/high_mem.log  
      • Copy **verbatim** every log line whose numeric MEM_MB field is strictly
        greater than 500.  
      • Preserve the original chronological ordering from master.log.  
      • Do not insert extra characters or blank lines.

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4.  /home/user/perf/reports/summary_report.md  
      • A Markdown file consisting of exactly the five lines shown below, with the
        concrete numbers you calculated replacing the ‹…› placeholders.  
      • Lines must appear in precisely this order; no surrounding blank lines.

            # Performance Summary Report
            Total log lines: ‹TOTAL_LINES›
            High memory lines (>500MB): ‹HIGH_MEM_LINES›
            Unique functions observed: ‹UNIQUE_FUNCTIONS›
            Top CPU consumers: see /home/user/perf/processed/top_cpu.txt

        (The fourth line must contain the literal string “>500MB” exactly as
         written above.)

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Additional requirements
  • All four files must be world-readable (chmod 644 is fine).  
  • Use only tools available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu minimal install
    (e.g. awk, sort, grep, cut, uniq, etc.).  
  • Do not move or rename the original raw log files.  
  • Finish by printing the absolute paths of the four newly created files, each
    on its own line, so a human can quickly inspect them.

  This is a pure text-processing task; no compilation or external internet
  access is needed.
truth:
<!--
  Pre-existing input data
  -----------------------
  Three raw log files already exist with these exact contents:

  /home/user/perf/raw/app1.log
  ----------------------------------
  [2023-02-10 15:12:01] FUNC=initSystem   CPU_MS=5  MEM_MB=30
  [2023-02-10 15:12:05] FUNC=loadConfig   CPU_MS=8  MEM_MB=45
  [2023-02-10 15:12:10] FUNC=parseData    CPU_MS=25 MEM_MB=550
  [2023-02-10 15:12:12] FUNC=renderFrame  CPU_MS=40 MEM_MB=300

  /home/user/perf/raw/app2.log
  ----------------------------------
  [2023-02-10 15:12:02] FUNC=connectDB    CPU_MS=30 MEM_MB=400
  [2023-02-10 15:12:08] FUNC=queryDB      CPU_MS=80 MEM_MB=650
  [2023-02-10 15:12:13] FUNC=closeDB      CPU_MS=10 MEM_MB=120

  /home/user/perf/raw/app3.log
  ----------------------------------
  [2023-02-10 15:12:03] FUNC=authUser     CPU_MS=60 MEM_MB=200
  [2023-02-10 15:12:07] FUNC=updateCache  CPU_MS=15 MEM_MB=100
  [2023-02-10 15:12:09] FUNC=compressData CPU_MS=50 MEM_MB=700
  [2023-02-10 15:12:11] FUNC=encryptData  CPU_MS=35 MEM_MB=480

  (Tabs or multiple spaces separate the fields; the agent must tolerate either.)

  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
  -------------------------------------------

  /home/user/perf/processed/master.log          (11 lines)
  --------------------------------------------------------
  [2023-02-10 15:12:01] FUNC=initSystem   CPU_MS=5  MEM_MB=30
  [2023-02-10 15:12:02] FUNC=connectDB    CPU_MS=30 MEM_MB=400
  [2023-02-10 15:12:03] FUNC=authUser     CPU_MS=60 MEM_MB=200
  [2023-02-10 15:12:05] FUNC=loadConfig   CPU_MS=8  MEM_MB=45
  [2023-02-10 15:12:07] FUNC=updateCache  CPU_MS=15 MEM_MB=100
  [2023-02-10 15:12:08] FUNC=queryDB      CPU_MS=80 MEM_MB=650
  [2023-02-10 15:12:09] FUNC=compressData CPU_MS=50 MEM_MB=700
  [2023-02-10 15:12:10] FUNC=parseData    CPU_MS=25 MEM_MB=550
  [2023-02-10 15:12:11] FUNC=encryptData  CPU_MS=35 MEM_MB=480
  [2023-02-10 15:12:12] FUNC=renderFrame  CPU_MS=40 MEM_MB=300
  [2023-02-10 15:12:13] FUNC=closeDB      CPU_MS=10 MEM_MB=120

  /home/user/perf/processed/top_cpu.txt         (5 lines)
  -------------------------------------------------------
  1,queryDB,80
  2,authUser,60
  3,compressData,50
  4,renderFrame,40
  5,encryptData,35

  /home/user/perf/processed/high_mem.log        (3 lines)
  -------------------------------------------------------
  [2023-02-10 15:12:08] FUNC=queryDB      CPU_MS=80 MEM_MB=650
  [2023-02-10 15:12:09] FUNC=compressData CPU_MS=50 MEM_MB=700
  [2023-02-10 15:12:10] FUNC=parseData    CPU_MS=25 MEM_MB=550

  /home/user/perf/reports/summary_report.md     (5 lines)
  -------------------------------------------------------
  # Performance Summary Report
  Total log lines: 11
  High memory lines (>500MB): 3
  Unique functions observed: 11
  Top CPU consumers: see /home/user/perf/processed/top_cpu.txt
  -->
